name: Ridge Regression (L2 regularization)
applicable_scenarios: linear regression when predictors are strongly correlated
stabilize coefficients under small n / large p
reduce variance at the cost of a small bias
python_template:
import numpy as np
from sklearn.linear_model import Ridge

def ridge_fit(X: np.ndarray, y: np.ndarray, alpha: float = 1.0) -> Ridge:
    model = Ridge(alpha=alpha)
    model.fit(X, y)
    return model
typical_cases: genomic or sensor arrays with many correlated features
common_pitfalls: requires feature scaling; penalty is unit-dependent
alpha chosen without cross-validation can under- or over-regularize
does not produce exactly-zero coefficients (use Lasso for selection)
